title: mise

oneOf.description: require this environment variable to be defined before mise runs or in a later config file
require this environment variable with user help text on how to set it

required.description: require this environment variable to be defined before mise runs or in a later config file. Cannot be used with empty string values or value=false. Can be a boolean or a help string.

oneOf.description: require this environment variable to be defined before mise runs or in a later config file
require this environment variable with user help text on how to set it

required.description: require this environment variable to be defined before mise runs or in a later config file. Cannot be used with empty string values or value=false. Can be a boolean or a help string.

format.description: [experimental] compression format for the encrypted value

oneOf.description: path to python virtual environment to use

create.description: create a new virtual environment if one does not exist

path.description: path to python virtual environment to use

python_create_args.description: additional arguments to pass to python when creating a virtual environment

uv_create_args.description: additional arguments to pass to uv when creating a virtual environment

activate_aggressive.description: Pushes tools' bin-paths to the front of PATH instead of allowing modifications of PATH after activation to take precedence.

identity_files.description: [experimental] List of age identity files to use for decryption.

key_file.description: [experimental] Path to the age private key file to use for encryption/decryption.

ssh_identity_files.description: [experimental] List of SSH identity files to use for age decryption.

strict.description: If true, fail when age decryption fails (including when age is not available, the key is missing, or the key is invalid). If false, skip decryption and continue in these cases.

all_compile.description: do not use precompiled binaries for any tool

always_keep_download.description: should mise keep downloaded files after installation

always_keep_install.description: should mise keep install files after installation even if the installation fails

cosign.description: Use cosign to verify aqua tool signatures.

cosign_extra_args.description: Extra arguments to pass to cosign when verifying aqua tool signatures.

github_attestations.description: Enable GitHub Artifact Attestations verification for aqua tools.

minisign.description: Use minisign to verify aqua tool signatures.

registry_url.description: URL to fetch aqua registry from.

slsa.description: Use SLSA to verify aqua tool signatures.

arch.description: Architecture to use for precompiled binaries.

asdf.description: use asdf as a default plugin backend

asdf_compat.description: set to true to ensure .tool-versions will be compatible with asdf

auto_install.description: Automatically install missing tools when running `mise x`, `mise run`, or as part of the 'not found' handler.

auto_install_disable_tools.description: List of tools to skip automatically installing when running `mise x`, `mise run`, or as part of the 'not found' handler.

cache_prune_age.description: Delete files in cache that have not been accessed in this duration

binstall.description: Use cargo-binstall instead of cargo install if available

binstall_only.description: Only use cargo-binstall for installation, fail if not available.

registry_name.description: Name of the cargo registry to use.

cargo_binstall.description: Use cargo-binstall instead of cargo install if available

cd.description: Path to change to after launching mise

ceiling_paths.description: Directories where mise stops searching for config files.

ci.description: Set to true if running in a CI environment

color.description: Use color in mise terminal output

color_theme.description: Theme for interactive prompts (default/charm, base16, catppuccin, dracula)

default_config_filename.description: The default config filename read. `mise use` and other commands that create new config files will use this value. This must be an env var.

default_tool_versions_filename.description: The default .tool-versions filename read. This will not ignore .tool-versions—use override_tool_versions_filename for that. This must be an env var.

disable_backends.description: Backends to disable such as `asdf` or `pipx`

disable_default_registry.description: Disable the default mapping of short tool names like `php` -> `asdf:mise-plugins/asdf-php`. This parameter disables only for the backends `vfox` and `asdf`.

disable_default_shorthands.description: Disables built-in shorthands to asdf/vfox plugins

disable_hints.description: Turns off helpful hints when using different mise features

disable_tools.description: Tools defined in mise.toml that should be ignored

package_flags.description: Extends dotnet search and install abilities.

registry_url.description: URL to fetch dotnet tools from.

enable_tools.description: Tools defined in mise.toml that should be used - all other tools are ignored

env.description: Env to use for mise.<MISE_ENV>.toml files.

env_cache.description: [experimental] Enable environment caching for nested mise invocations

env_file.description: Path to a file containing environment variables to automatically load.

env_shell_expand.description: Enable shell-style variable expansion in env values (e.g., $FOO, ${BAR:-default})

compile.description: If true, compile erlang from source. If false, use precompiled binaries. If not set, use precompiled binaries if available.

exec_auto_install.description: Automatically install missing tools when running `mise x`.

experimental.description: Enable experimental mise features which are incomplete or unstable—breakings changes may occur

fetch_remote_versions_cache.description: How long to cache remote versions for tools.

fetch_remote_versions_timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for HTTP requests to fetch new tool versions in mise.

github_attestations.description: Enable GitHub Artifact Attestations verification for github backend tools.

slsa.description: Enable SLSA provenance verification for github backend tools.

github_attestations.description: Enable GitHub Artifact Attestations verification for supported tools.

gix.description: Use gix for git operations, set to false to shell out to git.

global_config_file.description: Path to the global mise config file. Default is `~/.config/mise/config.toml`. This must be an env var.

global_config_root.description: Path which is used as `{{config_root}}` for the global config file. Default is `$HOME`. This must be an env var.

go_default_packages_file.description: Path to a file containing default go packages to install when installing go

go_download_mirror.description: Mirror to download go sdk tarballs from.

go_set_gobin.description: Changes where `go install` installs binaries to.

go_skip_checksum.description: Set to true to skip checksum verification when downloading go sdk tarballs.

gpg_verify.description: Use gpg to verify all tool signatures.

cache_ttl.description: Cache hook-env directory checks for this duration. Useful for slow filesystems like NFS.

chpwd_only.description: Only run hook-env checks on directory change, not on every prompt.

http_retries.description: Number of retries for HTTP requests in mise.

http_timeout.description: Timeout in seconds for all HTTP requests in mise.

idiomatic_version_file.description: Set to false to disable the idiomatic version files such as .node-version, .ruby-version, etc.

idiomatic_version_file_disable_tools.description: Specific tools to disable idiomatic version files for.

idiomatic_version_file_enable_tools.description: Specific tools to enable idiomatic version files for like .node-version, .ruby-version, etc.

ignored_config_paths.description: This is a list of config paths that mise will ignore.

install_before.description: Only install versions released before this date

shorthand_vendor.description: Shorthand for Java. Used when installing Java without a vendor prefix.

jobs.description: How many jobs to run concurrently such as tool installs.

legacy_version_file.description: Set to false to disable the idiomatic version files such as .node-version, .ruby-version, etc.

legacy_version_file_disable_tools.description: Specific tools to disable idiomatic version files for.

libgit2.description: Use libgit2 for git operations, set to false to shell out to git.

locked.description: Require lockfile URLs to be present during installation.

lockfile.description: Create and read lockfiles for tool versions.

netrc.description: Use a netrc file for HTTP Basic authentication.

netrc_file.description: Path to the netrc file to use for HTTP Basic authentication.

no_env.description: Do not load environment variables from config files.

no_hooks.description: Do not execute hooks from config files.

concurrency.description: How many jobs should be used in compilation.

corepack.description: Installs the default corepack shims after installing any node version.

default_packages_file.description: Path to a file containing default npm packages to install.

flavor.description: Install a specific node flavor like glibc-217 or musl. Use with unofficial node build repo.

gpg_verify.description: Use gpg to verify node tool signatures.

mirror_url.description: Mirror to download node tarballs from.

ninja.description: Use ninja instead of make to compile node.

verify.description: Verify the downloaded assets using GPG.

not_found_auto_install.description: Set to false to disable the "command not found" handler to autoinstall missing tool versions.

bun.description: Use bun instead of npm if bun is installed and on PATH.

package_manager.description: Package manager to use for installing npm packages.

offline.description: Disable all HTTP requests. Tools will only use locally cached data.

os.description: OS to use for precompiled binaries.

override_config_filenames.description: If set, mise will ignore default config files like `mise.toml` and use these filenames instead.

override_tool_versions_filenames.description: If set, mise will ignore .tool-versions files and use these filenames instead. Can be set to `none` to disable .tool-versions.

pin.description: Default to pinning versions when running `mise use` in mise.toml files.

registry_url.description: URL to use for pipx registry.

uvx.description: Use uvx instead of pipx if uv is installed and on PATH.

pipx_uvx.description: Use uvx instead of pipx if uv is installed and on PATH.

plugin_autoupdate_last_check_duration.description: How long to wait before updating plugins automatically (note this isn't currently implemented).

prefer_offline.description: Prefer locally cached data over remote fetches when possible.

profile.description: Profile to use for mise.${MISE_PROFILE}.toml files.

compile.description: If true, compile python from source. If false, use precompiled binaries. If not set, use precompiled binaries if available.

default_packages_file.description: Path to a file containing default python packages to install when installing a python version.

patch_url.description: URL to fetch python patches from to pass to python-build.

patches_directory.description: Directory to fetch python patches from.

precompiled_arch.description: Specify the architecture to use for precompiled binaries.

precompiled_flavor.description: Specify the flavor to use for precompiled binaries.

precompiled_os.description: Specify the OS to use for precompiled binaries.

pyenv_repo.description: URL to fetch pyenv from for compiling python with python-build.

uv_venv_auto.description: Integrate with uv to manage project venvs when uv.lock is present.

uv_venv_create_args.description: Arguments to pass to uv when creating a venv.

venv_auto_create.description: Automatically create virtualenvs for python tools.

venv_create_args.description: Arguments to pass to python when creating a venv. (not used for uv venv creation)

venv_stdlib.description: Prefer to use venv from Python's standard library.

python_compile.description: If true, compile python from source. If false, use precompiled binaries. If not set, use precompiled binaries if available.

python_default_packages_file.description: Path to a file containing default python packages to install when installing python.

python_patch_url.description: URL to fetch python patches from.

python_patches_directory.description: Directory to fetch python patches from.

python_precompiled_arch.description: Specify the architecture to use for precompiled binaries.

python_precompiled_os.description: Specify the OS to use for precompiled binaries.

python_pyenv_repo.description: URL to fetch pyenv from for compiling python.

python_venv_auto_create.description: Automatically create virtualenvs for python tools.

python_venv_stdlib.description: Prefer to use venv from Python's standard library.

apply_patches.description: A list of patch files or URLs to apply to ruby source.

compile.description: Set to true to compile ruby from source, false to use precompiled binaries.

default_packages_file.description: Path to a file containing default ruby gems to install when installing ruby.

github_attestations.description: Enable GitHub Artifact Attestations verification for precompiled Ruby binaries.

precompiled_arch.description: Override architecture identifier for precompiled Ruby binaries.

precompiled_os.description: Override OS identifier for precompiled Ruby binaries.

precompiled_url.description: URL template or GitHub repo for precompiled Ruby binaries.

ruby_build_repo.description: The URL used to fetch ruby-build. This accepts either a Git repository or a ZIP archive.

ruby_install_repo.description: The URL used to fetch ruby-install. This accepts either a Git repository or a ZIP archive.

verbose_install.description: Set to true to enable verbose output during ruby installation.

cargo_home.description: Path to the cargo home directory. Defaults to `~/.cargo` or `%USERPROFILE%\.cargo`

default_host.description: Default host triple to pass to `rustup init` via `--default-host`.

rustup_home.description: Path to the rustup home directory. Defaults to `~/.rustup` or `%USERPROFILE%\.rustup`

shorthands_file.description: Path to a file containing custom tool shorthands.

silent.description: Suppress all `mise run|watch` output except errors—including what tasks output.

age_key.description: The age private key to use for sops secret decryption. Takes precedence over standard SOPS_AGE_KEY environment variable.

age_key_file.description: Path to the age private key file for sops secret decryption. Takes precedence over standard SOPS_AGE_KEY_FILE environment variable.

age_recipients.description: The age public keys to use for sops secret encryption.

rops.description: Use rops to decrypt sops files. Disable to shell out to `sops` which will slow down mise but sops may offer features not available in rops.

strict.description: If true, fail when sops decryption fails (including when sops is not available, the key is missing, or the key is invalid). If false, skip decryption and continue in these cases.

missing_tools.description: Show a warning if tools are not installed when entering a directory with a mise.toml file.

show_env.description: Show configured env vars when entering a directory with a mise.toml file.

show_prepare_stale.description: Show warning when prepare providers have stale dependencies.

show_tools.description: Show configured tools when entering a directory with a mise.toml file.

gpg_verify.description: Use gpg to verify swift tool signatures.

platform.description: Override the platform to use for precompiled binaries.

system_config_file.description: Path to the system mise config file. Default is `/etc/mise/config.toml`. This must be an env var.

disable_paths.description: Paths that mise will not look for tasks in.

disable_spec_from_run_scripts.description: Opt out of parsing task run scripts to infer the usage spec (arguments and flags). When enabled, mise will derive the usage spec only from the `usage` field, ignoring any `arg()`, `option()`, or `flag()` templates used in run scripts. This can restore previous behavior and avoid the extra template pass over run scripts when collecting specs.

monorepo_depth.description: Maximum depth to search for task files in monorepo subdirectories.

monorepo_exclude_dirs.description: Directory patterns to exclude when discovering monorepo subdirectories.

monorepo_respect_gitignore.description: Whether to respect .gitignore files when discovering monorepo subdirectories.

output.description: Change output style when executing tasks.

remote_no_cache.description: Mise will always fetch the latest tasks from the remote, by default the cache is used.

run_auto_install.description: Automatically install missing tools when executing tasks.

show_full_cmd.description: Disable truncation of command lines in task execution output. When true, the full command line will be shown.

skip.description: Tasks to skip when running `mise run`.

skip_depends.description: Run only specified tasks skipping all dependencies.

source_freshness_equal_mtime_is_fresh.description: When source mtime equals output mtime, consider sources fresh (use <=). Default false uses strict < comparison.

source_freshness_hash_contents.description: Use content hashing (blake3) instead of metadata for source freshness. More accurate but slower.

timeout.description: Default timeout for tasks. Can be overridden by individual tasks.

timings.description: Show completion message with elapsed time for each task on `mise run`. Default shows when output type is `prefix`.

task_disable_paths.description: Paths that mise will not look for tasks in.

task_output.description: Change output style when executing tasks.

task_remote_no_cache.description: Mise will always fetch the latest tasks from the remote, by default the cache is used.

task_run_auto_install.description: Automatically install missing tools when executing tasks.

task_show_full_cmd.description: Disable truncation of command lines in task execution output. When true, the full command line will be shown.

task_skip.description: Tasks to skip when running `mise run`.

task_skip_depends.description: Run only specified tasks skipping all dependencies.

task_timeout.description: Default timeout for tasks. Can be overridden by individual tasks.

task_timings.description: Show completion message with elapsed time for each task on `mise run`. Default shows when output type is `prefix`.

terminal_progress.description: Enable terminal progress indicators (OSC 9;4) for compatible terminals.

trusted_config_paths.description: This is a list of config paths that mise will automatically mark as trusted.

unix_default_file_shell_args.description: Default shell arguments for Unix to be used for file commands. For example, `sh` for sh.

unix_default_inline_shell_args.description: Default shell arguments for Unix to be used for inline commands. For example, `sh -c` for sh.

url_replacements.description: Map of URL patterns to replacement URLs applied to all requests.

use_file_shell_for_executable_tasks.description: Determines whether to use a specified shell for executing tasks in the tasks directory. When set to true, the shell defined in the file will be used, or the default shell specified by `windows_default_file_shell_args` or `unix_default_file_shell_args` will be applied. If set to false, tasks will be executed directly as programs.

use_versions_host.description: Set to false to disable using the mise-versions API as a quick way for mise to query for new versions.

use_versions_host_track.description: Send anonymous download statistics when installing tools.

verbose.description: Shows more verbose output such as installation logs when installing tools.

vfox.description: Use vfox as a default plugin backend instead of asdf.

windows_default_file_shell_args.description: Default shell arguments for Windows to be used for file commands. For example, `cmd /c` for cmd.exe.

windows_default_inline_shell_args.description: Default shell arguments for Windows to be used for inline commands. For example, `cmd /c` for cmd.exe.

windows_executable_extensions.description: List of executable extensions for Windows. For example, `exe` for .exe files, `bat` for .bat files, and so on.

windows_shim_mode.description: Shim file mode for Windows. Options: `exe`, `file`, `hardlink`, `symlink`.

yes.description: This will automatically answer yes or no to prompts. This is useful for scripting.

task.description: single task name (with optional args) to run

confirm.description: confirmation message before running this task

oneOf.description: task with args to run before this task
task with args to run before this task
task with args to run after this task
task with args to run after this task
task with args to wait for completion first
task with args to wait for completion first

dir.description: directory to run script in, default is the project's base directory

tools.description: tools to install/activate before running this task

oneOf.description: version of the tool to install

version.description: version of the tool to install

items.description: glob pattern or path to files created by this task

oneOf.description: glob pattern or path to files created by this task

auto.description: automatically touch an internal tracked file instead of specifying outputs

quiet.description: do not display mise information for this task

silent.description: suppress all output for this task

oneOf.description: files that this task depends on

items.description: glob pattern or path to files that this task depends on

oneOf.description: glob pattern or path to files that this task depends on

shell.description: specify a shell command to run the script with

usage.description: Specify usage (https://usage.jdx.dev/) specs for the task

extends.description: name of the task template to extend

dir.description: default directory to run tasks in defined in this file

includes.description: files/directories to include searching for tasks. Can be local paths or git repository URLs using git:: prefix (e.g., git::https://github.com/org/repo.git//path?ref=branch)

items.description: file/directory root to include in task execution. Supports local paths and git URLs (git::https://github.com/org/repo.git//path?ref=branch)

experimental_monorepo_root.description: Marks this config as a monorepo root, enabling target path syntax for tasks. Requires MISE_EXPERIMENTAL=1. When enabled, tasks can be referenced with paths like //projects/frontend:build

config_roots.description: Explicit list of config root paths for monorepo task discovery. Supports single-level glob patterns (*). When set, skips filesystem walking for better performance.

oneOf.description: version of the tool to install

version.description: version of the tool to install

postinstall.description: command to run after tool installation

shell.description: specify the shell to run the script inside of
specify the shell to run the script inside of

run.description: script to run when file changes

outputs.description: Files/directories that should be newer than sources

confirm.description: confirmation message before running this task

oneOf.description: task with args to run before this task
task with args to run before this task

description: config file for mise version manager (mise.toml)

experimental_monorepo_root.description: marks this config as a monorepo root for task path syntax

min_version.description: minimum version of mise required to use this config

hard.description: error if current mise is lower than this version

soft.description: warn if current mise is lower than this version

redactions.description: env or vars keys to redact from logs

task_config.description: configuration for task execution and management

task_templates.description: task templates that can be extended by tasks via extends

hooks.description: hooks to run on events like cd, enter, leave

vars.description: variables to set for use in config templates

watch_files.description: files to watch for changes and run scripts when modified
